hypothesis_en: Mr President, today, as International Women's Day, I would like to call on this House not to talk about discrimination, because my colleagues have not spoken about it, but I believe that there is a problem against gender violence all over the world and especially in my country, and I think that both institutions and society must be aware that this must be solved.
reference_en: thank you very much (1.57) President today is International Women's Day (2.21) and I'd like to address (1.06) the plenary not talking about discrimination because other people have already talked about that (1.16) but I do feel there is one issue (1.75) gender violence in particular in my country and we see institutions and society (1.2) needing to become more aware of how we do need to find a solution to the issue of violence and I've asked for one minute's silence to be respected for the thirteen women who have died in Spain (1.28) in addition to those who have been murdered throughout the world thank you very much
source_es: gracias Señor Presidente hoy también como Día Internacional de la Mujer yo querría hacer un llamamiento a este plenario no voy a hablar ni de la discriminación porque ya la habla= lo han hablado anteriores compañeras mías pero sí que creo que hay un problema contra la violencia de género en todo el mundo y ~espencialmente en mi Estado y creo que tanto las instituciones como la sociedad tienen que tomar conciencia de que esto se tiene que solucionar y yo pediría a este plenario por eso he pedido la la palabra un minuto de silencio por las trece mujeres que ya llevan muertas en España más el resto que han que han sido asesinadas en todo el mundo gracias
